title: CompressorEffect
description: Dynamic-range compressor backed by a Web Audio `DynamicsCompressorNode`. Reduces the volume of loud signals above a threshold, evening out the perceived loudness of a mix. All parameter changes are applied with a short smoothing ramp so they are artifact-free during playback.

sections.title: Import
paragraphs: Dynamic-range compressor backed by a Web Audio `DynamicsCompressorNode`. Reduces the volume of loud signals above a threshold, evening out the perceived loudness of a mix. All parameter changes are applied with a short smoothing ramp so they are artifact-free during playback.

members.name: destroy
members.description: Disconnects all audio nodes and releases resources. Must be called when the effect is no longer needed.

members.name: attack
members.description: Time in seconds for the compressor to engage after the signal exceeds the threshold. Range 0..1, default 0.003.

members.name: inputNode
members.description: The node where audio enters this effect.

members.name: knee
members.description: Width in dB of the transition region around the threshold (soft knee). Range 0..40, default 30.

members.name: outputNode
members.description: The node where audio exits this effect.

members.name: ratio
members.description: Input-to-output dB ratio above the threshold. Range 1..20, default 12.

members.name: ready
members.description: Resolves when the effect is fully initialized. Defaults to immediately-resolved for sync effects.

members.name: reduction
members.description: Live gain reduction in dB applied by the compressor right now. Always <= 0 (or 0 when the compressor isn't engaging). Read-only — drive input level meters / sidechain visualisations off this value. Returns 0 until the underlying node has been created (before the audio context is ready).

members.name: release
members.description: Time in seconds for the compressor to disengage after the signal drops below the threshold. Range 0..1, default 0.25.

members.name: threshold
members.description: Level above which compression begins, in dBFS. Range −100..0, default −24.
